pattern: Whether missing dice observations can exist at all comes down to one range check on the total they must sum to, and any array that hits that sum with every value in range is equally valid.
hints: Work out the exact total the n missing rolls must add up to before worrying about the individual values.
A length-n array of values from 1 to 6 can reach any total between n and 6n and nothing outside that range, so check feasibility against that window first.

Let needed = mean*(m+n) - sum(rolls); if it falls outside [n, 6n] return an empty array. Otherwise let base = needed // n and extra = needed % n, then output extra copies of (base+1) followed by (n - extra) copies of base.

edge_inputs.description: unique feasible solution, both missing rolls forced to the max
needed sum exceeds the maximum possible, infeasible
needed sum below the minimum possible, infeasible
needed sum above the maximum possible for a single missing roll
minimum size on every parameter, feasible
larger n with a remainder that must be spread across slots
all missing rolls need the minimum value

stress.description: random rolls, mean, and missing count across the full constraint range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    m = max(1, min(size, 5000))
    n = rng.randint(1, max(1, min(size, 5000)))
    rolls = [rng.randint(1, 6) for _ in range(m)]
    mean = rng.randint(1, 6)
    return [rolls, mean, n]
